title: podman run - order of arguments makes the command fail/work
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--

VSCode Remote Containers uses a container to develop your code.
One of the steps in the process is to do a run of the container.
Before VSCode Remote containers used docker to do `docker run ...` and it works
After i have configured podman in VSCode Remote the command is `podman run ...` and it fails.
After investigating it looks like the order of the arguments for podman is important.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

- I installed podman v3.4.1 
- configured "podman" executable in VSCode Remote Containers https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/remote/containers#_can-i-use-podman-instead-of-docker
- rebuild container (now by using podman) and i get the following error:

`➜ ~ podman run --sig-proxy=false -a STDOUT -a STDERR --mount type=bind,source=/Users/vincentclaes/Workspace/daw-workflow-demo,target=/workspaces/daw-workflow-demo,consistency=cached --mount type=volume,src=vscode,dst=/vscode -l vsch.local.folder=/Users/vincentclaes/Workspace/daw-workflow-demo -l vsch.quality=stable -l vsch.remote.devPort=0 --entrypoint /bin/sh vsc-daw-workflow-demo-3f7c7397d809af749879c9f70184b337 -c echo Container started`




**Describe the results you received:**


`*Error* : statfs /Users/vincentclaes/Workspace/daw-workflow-demo: no such file or directory`

- it looks like the error is related to my project folder that needs to be mounted to the container. While browsing google and SO i came to this [thread](https://stackoverflow.com/a/51629165/1771155 ) where they say the order of the arguments might be causing the issue. 

**Describe the results you expected:**

- Reorganizing the position of the parameters should not be an issue, and reordering them in VScode remote containers is not feasible afaik.
- When I reorder the command by putting the "mount" parameters to the back of the command, and execute the command from the terminal, I do not get an error anymore. 

`➜ ~ podman run --sig-proxy=false -a STDOUT -a STDERR -l vsch.local.folder=/Users/vincentclaes/Workspace/daw-workflow-demo -l vsch.quality=stable -l vsch.remote.devPort=0 --entrypoint /bin/sh vsc-daw-workflow-demo-3f7c7397d809af749879c9f70184b337 -c echo Container started --mount type=bind,source=/Users/vincentclaes/Workspace/daw-workflow-demo,target=/workspaces/daw-workflow-demo,consistency=cached --mount type=volume,src=vscode,dst=/vscode`




**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Go Version:   go1.16.8

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64```
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

MacBook Pro + VSCode + Remote Containers Plugin

comments.body: Volumes are not supported with MAC yet.  Podman is attempting to mount this directory from within the VM not from the MAC. So it is failing.  We are figuring out the best way to handle this upstream and should have a fix by the end of the year.

The ordering matter but only if you add arguments behind the image name. Every argument behind the image will be added to the container entrypoint and it not interpreted by podman.

The remote volume mount work is tracked here https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8016 I believe .

> Volumes are not supported with MAC yet. Podman is attempting to mount this directory from within the VM not from the MAC. So it is failing. We are figuring out the best way to handle this upstream and should have a fix by the end of the year.

I can't use volume with Mac.

```

4.1.0, just released, will be the first version with volumes support.
Well not quite correct.  First with volumes defaulted.
